title: When the Mind Wants Drama
summary: In this episode of Always Connected, we look at something every one of us does without realizing it: the mind’s tendency to turn ordinary situations into dramatic stories. A comment, a delay, a misunderstanding—and suddenly the mind is off and running, filling in motives, predicting outcomes, and stirring up emotions that may have nothing to do with what’s actually happening.  When we learn to recognize this pattern, something important shifts. We don’t have to suppress the story or fight with it; we simply see it for what it is. From that clearer space, we can respond with far more wisdom and far less stress. Join me as we explore how to notice when the story is trying to take over and how staying present keeps you connected to guidance, clarity, and the deeper awareness that is always available.Suzanne Giesemann is a former Navy Commander-turned spiritual teacher, author, documentarian and evidential medium. title: Stop Waiting for Perfect: Starting Before You're Ready | Solo Episode
summary: In this first solo episode, Amber Doyle shares a raw and honest reflection on perfectionism, procrastination, and the courage it takes to finally begin something you've been putting off. Recording from her bed while her baby sleeps, Amber opens up about the pressure we place on ourselves to have everything perfectly prepared before we take action — and why waiting for the “perfect moment” can keep us stuck. Drawing on tarot insights and the energetic shift into a new cycle, Amber explores the themes of releasing old patterns, stepping out of fear, and embracing the opportunities that come when we finally trust ourselves enough to move forward. She also speaks about the collective energy of transition, that in-between space where one chapter is closing and another is just beginning. and why patience, reflection, and listening to your intuition are so important during this time. This episode is a reminder that you don’t have to have everything figured out to begin. Sometimes the most powerful step forward is simply deciding to start. If you’ve been holding yourself back from something you feel called to do, this conversation may be the sign you’ve been waiting for. summary: Sleep deprivation is one of the most challenging and isolating parts of becoming a parent,c yet so many parents feel like they’re the only ones struggling. Together, Amber and Lucy explore the emotional and practical sides of parenting, including the pressure parents place on themselves and how understanding your child’s sleep can transform the experience of early parenthood. title: From Streets to Soul: Breaking Barriers, Owning Space & Being Seen | Finbar McHugh
summary: What if creativity isn’t about talent… but about truth? In this expansive and deeply reflective conversation, Amber Doyle sits down with Galway-based artist and soul painter Finbar McHugh to explore the connection between creativity, identity, and self-awareness. Finbar shares how creativity has been both an escape and a return: a place of refuge, and a place of radical self-confrontation. From childhood doodling to intuitive soul paintings, he speaks openly about the courage it takes to create something that reflects who you truly are. Because creativity is not passive. It is a mirror. And when we allow ourselves to create, there is nowhere to hide. Together, Amber and Finbar explore: Why everyone is inherently creative, and why many lose access to itHow fear shapes our self-expressionCreativity as self-inquiry and emotional awarenessThe vulnerability of being seen through your workSoul paintings and intuitive art as energetic reflectionThe tension between safety and authenticity This episode gently challenges the belief that creativity belongs to “artists.” Instead, it invites you to see it as a birthright, one that asks for courage, honesty, and self-trust. title: Visibility, Vulnerability & the Artist’s Journey | Julianne Guinee
summary: In this episode, Amber sits down with Irish oil painter Julianne Guinee, whose deeply emotive figurative works explore the unseen emotional and cultural pressures shaping women’s lives. Based in Buttevant, County Cork, Julianne left her teaching career in 2019 to pursue painting full time, a decision that required courage, trust, and a willingness to finally call herself an artist. Together, Amber and Julianne dive into the inner world behind her paintings: the tension and tenderness of domestic spaces, the expectations placed on women, and the quiet contradictions that shape our lives. Julianne speaks openly about the fear of visibility, the discomfort of being truly seen, and how learning to believe in herself changed the trajectory of her career. She shares the practical and emotional realities of being a working artist in Ireland, from exhibitions and rejections to confidence-building milestones — and the transformative moment she was awarded the prestigious Accenture Women on Walls commission to paint eight pioneering women for permanent display at University College Cork. This conversation is a powerful exploration of creativity, identity, self-worth, and the messy, beautiful unfolding of purpose. 🌿 Connect with Julianne: Website Instagram Recorded as part of ALIGNED | The Path to Purpose with Amber Doyle Thank you for listening to ALIGNED |The Path to Purpose. title: Understanding the Masks we Wear (Satya Doyle Byock)
podcast_name: Pulling The Thread with Elise Loehnen
summary: “We’re in a time of masking and masks, whether we know it or not,” says psychotherapist Satya Doyle Byock. “And my hope is that us talking about it draws that into greater consciousness for people to make decisions about when to put them on and when to take them off.” Today, we’re talking about persona, in the sense of the masks we wear for various reasons (some beneficial, some nefarious). And, how, in becoming attached to the idea of authenticity and being in a rush to identify someone else or ourselves as inauthentic—we can really miss our mark. title: From Loss to Light: Reawakening the Goddess Within | Catherine Eager
summary: In this moving episode of ALIGNED | The Path to Purpose, Amber Doyle sits down with visionary artist and creative entrepreneur Catherine Eager, the founder of Goddess Ireland; a brand born from grief, love, and spiritual rebirth. Catherine opens up about the tragic and sudden loss of her sister, followed soon after by the passing of her dear mother: two life-shattering moments that transformed her path forever. Through her healing journey, she discovered the power of creativity as a sacred medicine, blending art, nature, and spirit to reconnect with the divine feminine. Today, through Goddess Ireland, Catherine channels that healing into hand-painted goddess portraits, botanical skincare, and soulful rituals that help others reconnect with their inner goddess and self-worth. Together, Amber and Catherine explore how pain can be a portal to purpose, and how beauty, when rooted in spirit, becomes a form of remembrance, empowerment, and homecoming to the self. title: An Episode from Other People’s Problems: On Psychedelics
podcast_name: Pulling The Thread with Elise Loehnen
summary: Here, I'm sharing another podcast series with you, called Other People’s Problems. In her new season, host and therapist Hillary McBride explores the potential power of psychedelics in a therapeutic setting. She leads clients through drug-assisted therapy—and we'll experience these real, unscripted sessions as they unfold, and get a more honest look at therapy using psychedelics. Which I hope helps to further demystify this often misunderstood practice and tool in trauma recovery. In this episode, we hear from Donovan, who has lived in fear and anger ever since telling the truth about being abused as a child. Now, after several ketamine therapy sessions, Donovan is able to look back at his young self with care. And, for his own children, Donovan works to become the kind of adult he needed then.
